name: Potato Leek Soup
tagline: Silky French potato soup with sweet sweated leeks and cream
highlight: Three vegetables, silken texture, naturally nut-free

hero.alt: Bowl of potato leek soup with chive garnish and cream swirl
hero.caption: potato leek — silky and pale

healthFacts: Leeks are rich in inulin — prebiotic fiber that feeds gut bacteria.
Yukon gold potatoes blend smoother than russets thanks to their lower starch content.

steps.title: Clean and slice leeks
steps.detail: Trim leeks. Halve lengthwise and rinse out grit. Slice thin. Soak in cold water 5 minutes; lift out so dirt stays behind.
steps.tip: Leeks hide grit between layers. Two-stage washing saves the soup.

steps.title: Sweat the leeks
steps.detail: Melt butter over medium-low. Add leeks and a pinch of salt. Cook 10 minutes, stirring, until soft but not browned.
steps.tip: No color — you want sweet, not toasty.

steps.title: Simmer with potatoes
steps.detail: Add diced potatoes, stock, and bay leaf. Simmer 20 minutes until potatoes break apart with a fork.
steps.tip: Don't boil hard — broken cells release excess starch and turn the soup gluey.

steps.title: Blend and finish
steps.detail: Fish out bay leaf. Blend until silky. Stir in cream off heat. Adjust salt. Top with chives.
steps.tip: Immersion blender works fine; for restaurant-smooth, pass through a fine sieve.
